title: lepechinia calycina var. glabella
intro: Lepechinia calycina var. glabella, also known as the Desert Lavender or Narrow-leaved Mountain Hyssop, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the mint family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lepechinia calycina var. glabella include reducing stress and anxiety, improving digestion, and promoting relaxation, thanks to its calming and soothing effects. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken internally or applied topically to the skin to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
